no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states: 'Mark HandleCurrentExceptionWhenLeavingKotlinCode with the RUNTIME_NORETURN attribute in all relevant locations' and the Validation section spells out exactly what to do in each of the three files. This is the complete solution, not just a problem description.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use exact regex patterns that enforce specific placement of RUNTIME_NORETURN. For example, test_exceptions_h_has_noreturn requires 'void RUNTIME_NORETURN HandleCurrentExceptionWhenLeavingKotlinCode()' but a developer might place it as 'RUNTIME_NORETURN void HandleCurrentExceptionWhenLeavingKotlinCode()' (which is how __attribute__((noreturn)) is commonly used in GCC/Clang and how ThrowException is declared). The test would fail for this equally correct alternative placement.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests (test_exceptions_h_has_noreturn, test_exceptions_cpp_has_noreturn, test_c_adapter_exporter_has_noreturn) and most structural tests are purely text-matching with regex on file contents. No test compiles the code or verifies the warning is actually suppressed. The subprocess tests (clang, git) are pass-to-pass only.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for RUNTIME_NORETURN being present in specific patterns that don't exist in the original files, so a stub/no-op solution would not pass them.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction's three validation criteria (RUNTIME_NORETURN in .h declaration, .cpp definition, and .kt generated code) are all tested by the fail-to-pass tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear names, comments separating FAIL-TO-PASS, STRUCTURAL, PASS-TO-PASS, and STATIC categories, and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves source code edits, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, function names, macro names, and commands appear correct and consistent across instruction and test files.
